Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a product in the PuroRush catalog.

name.description: Name of the product (e.g., Rush Winter 10ml).

id.description: Unique identifier for the shopping cart.

orderDate.description: Date and time the order was placed.

stripeTransactionId.description: The transaction ID returned from Stripe

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the PuroRush app.  Authentication data (passwords, etc.) are assumed to be handled by an external system like Firebase Auth and are NOT stored in this entity.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product.

definition.description: Stores shopping cart information for each user.  Accessible only by the user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: cartId
params.description: The unique identifier of the shopping cart.

definition.description: Stores order information for each user. Accessible only by the user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Accessible only by the user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure Authorization Independence, support QAPs (queries are not filters), and provide clarity for security rules. It leverages structural segregation to maintain a homogeneous security posture across collections.

Products are stored in a global collection (`/products`) with public read access. User-specific data, such as shopping carts and orders, are stored in path-based collections under `/users/{userId}`, providing implicit ownership. This eliminates the need for complex `get()` calls in security rules and enables atomic operations.

QAPs are supported by avoiding mixing data with different access needs in the same collection. Products are publicly accessible, while shopping carts and orders are private to each user. This simplifies `list` operations and prevents unauthorized data access.
